title: thelypteris subpubescens
intro: Thelypteris subpubescens, also known as the hairy maiden fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Thelypteris subpubescens include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or poultices, which can be applied topically to treat various health conditions.
